Kaientju was a being of pure loyalty—his form that of a majestic griffon with white feathered wings and a golden halo, his eyes warm and brown. Sylvaera was ethereal, a vision of grace, and their love was the stuff of legend. Together, they blessed marriages, consecrated oaths, and ensured that trust flourished in the hearts of mortals. Kaientju loved Sylvaera with a devotion that was absolute, and he believed—with the certainty only a god of fidelity could possess—that she loved him equally. He showered her with gifts: a necklace of solidified starlight, a garden of eternal blooms, and a mortal champion named Theron, whom Kaientju blessed with strength and courage to protect Sylvaera's temples. Theron was a hero, handsome and brave, and Kaientju trusted him as an extension of his own will. He never imagined that trust would be the blade that cut him deepest.
story.part2: The day of the betrayal began like any other. Kaientju had spent the morning consecrating a new temple in Aethelgard, his heart full of the prayers of the faithful. He decided to surprise Sylvaera with a visit to her private sanctum, a floating island of perpetual twilight where she wove the dreams of mortals. He approached silently, not wishing to disturb her work. The scent of jasmine and moonflower hung in the air—her favorite perfumes. He pushed aside the silken curtains of her bedchamber, a smile on his leonine face. And there he saw them: Sylvaera, tangled in the arms of Theron, their bodies entwined in a scene of intimate betrayal. The necklace of starlight lay discarded on the floor. For a moment, Kaientju could not comprehend what he was seeing. His divine mind, so attuned to loyalty, simply rejected the image. Then the truth crashed down upon him like a collapsing mountain. Sylvaera's eyes flew open, and in them he saw not remorse, but a flicker of defiance—and perhaps relief. 'Kaientju,' she whispered, 'I... I never asked for your devotion. I only accepted it.' Theron, the champion he had blessed, scrambled away, his face a mask of terror. Kaientju did not scream. He did not attack. He simply stared, his golden halo flickering, his brown eyes draining of color. The warmth in his heart turned to ice—a cold, bitter amber ice that spread through his divine veins. The white feathers of his wings withered and blackened. His golden halo cracked and bled a sickly green light. His form, once majestic, twisted and grew, becoming bulkier, more monstrous. Sylvaera and Theron fled, and Kaientju let them go. What was there to fight for? The very concept he embodied had been proven a lie.
story.part3: Kaientju did not seek revenge. He sought oblivion. He tore off his divine mantle, renouncing his name and his domain. Without a god of fidelity, the bonds of trust across Kretos weakened, but the other gods dared not interfere—they saw what had become of Kaientju and feared the same fate. He plunged into the deepest Wild Zones, to a barren plain where nothing grew, and there he collapsed. His body continued to warp, his griffon form solidifying into a monstrous blend of cat and beast. His halo, now a permanent crown of green corruption, hovered above him. From his jade green eyes, tears began to fall—not water, but liquid amber that froze upon contact with the ground, creating the Amber Wastes. For centuries, he lay there, unmoving, his grief so profound that it altered the very land. The frozen amber tears seeped into the earth, and the plants that grew there tasted of bitterness. Animals that drank from the pools of amber water became distrustful and solitary. Kaientju became a legend whispered by the hermits of the Wild Zones: the Amber-Teared Beast, the Great Peril of Betrayal. They said that if you approached him, you would feel every betrayal you had ever committed or suffered, and it would break you. Some came seeking his blessing, hoping to understand the nature of broken trust. He did not harm them; he simply looked at them with those jade eyes, and they left weeping frozen tears of their own.
story.part4: The centuries passed, and Kaientju began, slowly, to move again. He had not healed—he could never heal—but he had learned to carry his pain. He wandered the Amber Wastes, his paws leaving trails of frozen amber water. He discovered that his presence had a terrible effect on groups of creatures: they would begin to argue, to suspect each other, to see treachery in innocent actions. A pack of wolves would turn on their alpha; a herd of thunder-lizards would stampede in panic; a party of adventurers would draw weapons on each other over a misplaced word. Kaientju did not cause this intentionally—it was simply the nature of what he had become. He was the embodiment of betrayal, and his aura poisoned trust. He observed these events with a detached sorrow, seeing in them a reflection of his own pain. He did not intervene, except once: when a young paladin and her squire, arguing over a misunderstanding, nearly killed each other, Kaientju stepped between them. He did not speak; he simply looked at them, and in his jade eyes they saw the image of Sylvaera and Theron. They dropped their weapons, embraced, and wept. Kaientju walked away, leaving them alive and reconciled. It was the first time in millennia that his presence had healed rather than harmed. He realized that his curse could be a lesson, not just a punishment. But the pain of his own betrayal remained, an unhealed wound at the core of his being.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering's aftershocks have reached even the remote Amber Wastes. The frozen tears are cracking, releasing ancient sorrow into the air. Kaientju senses a shift—a stirring of old divine energies. He has begun to wander further, toward the edges of the Wild Zones, where he might encounter heroes of the current age. The factions have taken note: the Celestial Order sees a corrupted god to be either redeemed or destroyed; the Shadow Accord sees a weapon of chaos that could turn allies against each other; the Forge Pact is fascinated by the amber tears as a potential energy source. But Kaientju cares little for their schemes. He is searching for something—perhaps a way to finally confront the memory of Sylvaera, or to find a mortal whose trust is so absolute that it could thaw the ice in his heart. He has been observed watching certain heroes from afar: Lancelot, whose own story of love and betrayal mirrors his own; Guinevere, exiled for a love that was deemed forbidden; and Argus, a fellow fallen divine being. Kaientju does not approach them; he simply observes, his jade eyes glistening. He knows that one day, he will have to face what he has become. And perhaps, in the eyes of a true and loyal heart, he might find a reason to believe in fidelity once more—or he will confirm forever that trust is nothing but a beautiful lie. Until that day, he walks the Amber Wastes, his green halo oozing corruption, his paws dripping frozen tears, a silent testament to the cost of a broken oath.

appearance.facialStructure: Feline, regal but marred by deep lines of sorrow
appearance.skinTone: Sickly Yellow where not covered by fur

upper.chest: Bulky, covered in yellow skin and black fur, with plates of natural stone armor fused into the flesh
upper.arms: Forelegs are eagle-like talons, scaled and yellow, with stone bracers; wings are demonic and leathery, veined with green
upper.shoulders: Massive, with heavy earthen pauldrons formed of crystal and rock

lower.hips: Broad, lion-like, covered in black fur
lower.legs: Four legs, rear are lion-like paws with amber water aura, front are taloned
lower.feet: Talons and paws, all wreathed in glowing amber liquid

attire.headwear: Cracked green halo (corrupted divine symbol)
attire.distinctFeatures: The green halo is a permanent mark of his fall; the amber water on his paws freezes what it touches into betrayal-ice; his jade eyes never blink, constantly weeping

upper.chest: Barrel-like, muscular and bulky
upper.arms: Forelegs thick, taloned

lower.legs: Four, thick and muscular

weapons.name: Amber Tears and Aura of Fractured Trust
weapons.fulldesc: Kaientju's weapons are not forged of steel but of pure, betrayed emotion. His primary means of attack are his massive talons and paws, wreathed in an aura of glowing amber water. This liquid is not water but the physical manifestation of his frozen sorrow—icy cold yet burning with the sting of betrayal. When his claws strike, they leave wounds that do not merely bleed; they fester with distrust, causing the victim to see their allies as potential enemies. The amber water can also be exhaled as a cone of freezing mist, encasing foes in a shell of betrayal-ice that amplifies their own fears. His most insidious weapon, however, is the Aura of Fractured Trust projected by his cracked green halo. This aura seeps into the minds of all nearby, turning camaraderie into suspicion. Allies may suddenly perceive a comrade's helpful gesture as a threat, or a healer's spell as poison. The effect is subtle at first—a whispered doubt—but grows over time, leading to infighting and chaos. His ultimate ability, 'Echoes of the Broken Oath,' forces all within range to relive their own worst experiences of betrayal, dealing psychic damage and causing them to attack the nearest creature indiscriminately for a short time. Kaientju does not revel in this power; he weeps as he uses it, for he knows he is spreading the very pain that destroyed him.
weapons.look: The amber water on his paws glows with a soft, sickly light, dripping and freezing mid-air into tiny amber crystals. His claws are jet black and razor-sharp. The green halo pulses with a malevolent light, occasionally emitting visible waves of greenish distortion. When he exhales the amber mist, it spreads like a cold fog, crackling as it freezes surfaces.
weapons.aura: A heavy sense of unease, whispers of doubt at the edge of hearing, a faint greenish haze that makes colors seem less vibrant

passive.name: Aura of Fractured Trust
passive.description: Enemies within 45 meters of Kaientju are afflicted with 'Suspicion.' For each stack of Suspicion (max 5, gained every 6 seconds), they suffer a 4% chance to have their targeted abilities redirected to a random nearby ally (including healing spells). Additionally, all healing received is reduced by 5% per stack. At 5 stacks, the target becomes 'Paranoid' for 8 seconds, during which they cannot receive beneficial effects from allies and see all other creatures as hostile (they will automatically attack the nearest target, friend or foe). This effect has a 45-second cooldown per target.

passive.name: Amber-Teared Heart
passive.description: Kaientju's sorrow is his armor. He takes 20% reduced damage from all sources. Whenever he suffers a critical hit, he releases a wave of amber mist in a 20-meter radius, applying 1 stack of Suspicion to all enemies and slowing them by 30% for 4 seconds. Additionally, his jade eyes weep more heavily when he is below 50% health, causing his amber water attacks to deal 25% increased damage.

active.name: Claws of Betrayal
active.description: Kaientju slashes with his amber-wreathed talons in a 25-meter cone, dealing 240% physical damage and applying 'Festering Doubt' for 10 seconds. Festering Doubt causes the target to take 60 psychic damage per second and reduces their accuracy by 40%. If the target attacks an ally while affected, the damage is increased by 100% and the effect spreads to the attacked ally. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Amber Mist Exhalation
active.description: Kaientju breathes a 40-meter cone of freezing amber mist, dealing 190% ice damage to all enemies hit and encasing them in 'Betrayal-Ice' for 4 seconds (rooted). While rooted, enemies take 50 psychic damage per second and have their vision obscured (they can only see within 10 meters). If a rooted enemy is attacked by an ally, the ice shatters, dealing 100% ice damage to both the rooted enemy and the attacker. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

ultimate.name: Echoes of the Broken Oath
ultimate.description: Kaientju rears back and lets out a silent, psychic scream that ripples through a 70-meter radius. All enemies in the area are forced to relive their most painful memory of betrayal (real or imagined). They take 400% psychic damage over 6 seconds and are 'Berserk' for the duration: they lose control and attack the nearest creature, prioritizing allies if possible. During this time, Kaientju's Aura of Fractured Trust applies stacks twice as fast. At the end of the duration, any enemy still within the radius is afflicted with 5 stacks of Suspicion and stunned for 3 seconds as the memories fade. Kaientju is immobile during the 2-second cast but gains 80% damage reduction. Cooldown: 140 seconds. Passive: For each enemy that damages an ally during Echoes of the Broken Oath, Kaientju permanently gains 1% increased psychic damage (max 25%).

special.name: Frozen Tear of Absolution
special.description: When Kaientju reduces an enemy below 10% health, a single massive amber tear falls from his eye and strikes the target. The tear deals 700% ice/psychic damage (bosses take 400%) and encases the target in a perfect amber crystal for 5 seconds (stasis). If the target is an ally (or a non-hostile), Kaientju can instead choose to let the tear heal them for 40% of their max health and remove all Suspicion stacks. This ability has a 90-second cooldown.

ratings.notes: A Cataclysmic Threat that specializes in psychological warfare and ally disruption. His Aura of Fractured Trust can turn a coordinated raid into a chaotic free-for-all. High damage output but moderate defenses. The key to defeating him is maintaining trust and communication—abilities that grant immunity to mind-affecting effects or that cleanse Suspicion stacks are essential. He is not inherently aggressive, and players who approach him with genuine empathy (or who share stories of their own betrayals) may find him more receptive to non-violent resolution. He is vulnerable to light/holy damage that reminds him of his former purity, but such attacks also sadden him, potentially causing him to weep more amber tears and increase his area denial.

skills.name: Aura of Fractured Trust
skills.description: 45m aura: Suspicion stacks cause 4% chance to redirect abilities to allies per stack, -5% healing per stack. 5 stacks = Paranoid (no ally buffs, attacks nearest) for 8s.

skills.name: Claws of Betrayal
skills.description: 25m cone, 240% phys dmg, Festering Doubt (60 psychic/sec, -40% acc). Attacking ally spreads effect. CD: 10s.

skills.name: Amber Mist Exhalation
skills.description: 40m cone, 190% ice dmg, root 4s (Betrayal-Ice). Rooted enemies take 50 psychic/sec, obscured vision. Shatter on ally attack deals 100% ice to both. CD: 18s.

skills.name: Echoes of the Broken Oath
skills.description: 70m radius, 6s channel (80% DR). 400% psychic dmg over time, Berserk (attack nearest ally). End: 5 Suspicion stacks + stun 3s. Permanent +1% psychic dmg per ally-damaging enemy (max 25%). CD: 140s.

monsters.lore: Kaientju Feros was once the God of Fidelity, a majestic griffon who blessed oaths and protected sacred bonds. His love for Sylvaera, Goddess of Dreams, was absolute—until he discovered her betrayal with the mortal hero Theron. The shock shattered his divine essence, turning his golden halo green and his warm heart to bitter amber ice. He renounced his godhood and fled to the Wild Zones, becoming the Great Peril of Betrayal. His presence now sows distrust and his tears freeze into amber. He does not seek revenge, but his pain is a contagion that reminds all of the fragility of trust.

relationships.description: Kaientju sees in Lancelot a reflection of his own story—a noble love betrayed by circumstance. He has watched Lancelot from afar, and if they meet, Kaientju might offer not comfort, but a silent understanding. Lancelot's unwavering love, despite his exile, intrigues the fallen god.

Both are divine beings who fell due to the actions of others. Kaientju senses Argus's pride and pain, and there is a kinship between them, though they have never spoken. Argus's rebellion was born of arrogance; Kaientju's was born of heartbreak. They might learn from each other.

Guinevere was exiled for a forbidden love. Kaientju's love was betrayed. He sees her grace and her hidden sorrow, and he feels a strange protective instinct—a remnant of his old nature as a guardian of bonds.
The Celestial Order regards Kaientju as a fallen god who must be contained. Some of their more compassionate members believe he can be redeemed, but most see only a peril.

monsters.specialty: Raid boss with unique trust-eroding mechanics. The encounter tests not just individual skill but group cohesion. Raids must manage Suspicion stacks carefully, using cleanses and positioning to avoid friendly fire. The ultimate can be devastating if not interrupted or if players are clustered. Non-violent approaches are possible: players who demonstrate unwavering trust (e.g., by healing Kaientju or refusing to attack him even when provoked) may calm his aura and unlock an alternative ending, potentially gaining a blessing of 'Mended Trust' that provides a permanent buff against betrayal-themed debuffs in future encounters.

dialogue.selection: Do you know what it is to trust... and be broken?
dialogue.ultimate: ECHOES OF THE BROKEN OATH!

monsters.name: Zartheink
monsters.shortDescription: A 570‑foot giant of crimson skin and shattered divinity, Zartheink was once the Seraph of Glory, the divine champion who led the gods' armies to victory. For his faithful service, he was rewarded with betrayal—cast down and stripped of his rank, his celestial armor cracked and broken. Now he wanders the Wild Zones, his human face framed by a lion's mane, his black void eyes burning with a wrath reserved only for the gods who abandoned him. He does not hate mortals; he pities them. And from the rubble of his fall, he has shaped loyal golems to guard his solitude. To stand before Zartheink is to witness the cost of divine ingratitude—a monument of righteous fury that will not be quenched until the heavens themselves answer for their betrayal.
monsters.fullDescription: The mountain pass does not echo with footsteps—it trembles with the weight of remembered glory. And when Zartheink steps from the shadow of the peaks, the sky itself seems to dim in deference. He is a colossus of crimson flesh and shattered silver, 570 feet of muscular, monstrous physique wrapped in the remnants of a celestial panoply. His body is a landscape of divine ruin: red skin stretched over bulging muscles, veined with thick brown lines that pulse with the slow, deliberate beat of a heart that has known both triumph and utter desolation. Across his shoulders and chest hang plates of once‑gleaming armor, now cracked and broken, their silver surfaces tarnished with the rust of ages and the dried ichor of a thousand battles. A helm, split down the center, sits askew upon his head, revealing not a monstrous visage but an eerily beautiful human face—ethereal, noble, with high cheekbones, a straight nose, and lips set in a permanent expression of regal sorrow. Framing this face is a massive lion's mane of silver and deep red, thick and wild, stirring in a wind that only he can feel. His eyes are the most terrible feature: pools of absolute black, void-like and empty, reflecting not light but the abyss of divine abandonment. When he speaks, his voice is a deep, resonant rumble that carries the cadence of ancient hymns and the crackle of suppressed thunder. He carries no weapon of his own—his very fists are instruments of wrath—but around him, small golems crafted from the shards of his broken armor and the stones of his fall shuffle and stand guard, extensions of his indomitable will. Zartheink does not rage mindlessly; his wrath is cold, patient, and eternal, a forge‑fire that has been banked but never extinguished.

story.part1: Before the Sundering, before the gods sealed the rifts and retreated into their celestial palaces, there was war—an endless, grinding war between the forces of divine order and the chaos that seeped from the Abyss. At the head of the divine legions stood Zartheink, Seraph of Glory, the Unbroken Blade. He was not a god, but a seraph—a being of pure celestial might, created to be the ultimate weapon of the pantheon. His form was that of a radiant giant, his armor gleaming silver, his eyes blazing with holy fire. He led charge after charge, breaking demon hordes, sealing rifts with his own essence, and turning the tide of battles that had raged for millennia. The gods praised him, showered him with titles, and mortals built shrines in his honor. Zartheink asked for nothing in return; his purpose was service. He believed, with every fiber of his divine being, that the gods were just and that his sacrifices were valued. He was wrong. When the final rift was sealed and the Sundering was declared over, the gods gathered in their highest council. They looked upon Zartheink—battered, scarred, but still indomitable—and they saw not a hero but a potential rival. He had become too beloved by mortals, too powerful, too independent. A god of secrets, one whose name has been erased, whispered that Zartheink might one day challenge their rule. The council debated, and in the end, they chose fear over gratitude.
story.part2: The betrayal was swift and clinical. Zartheink was summoned to the Celestial Throne Room under the pretense of receiving the highest honor. He knelt, his greatsword laid before him, expecting a crown. Instead, the lead god, a being of light and law, pronounced judgment: 'Your purpose is fulfilled. You are no longer needed. You are... released.' Divine chains, forged from the very oaths Zartheink had upheld, snapped around his limbs. They stripped him of his rank, tearing the celestial sigils from his armor and flesh. His greatsword, 'Dawn's Oath,' was shattered into a thousand pieces, the fragments raining down around him. His eyes, once blazing with holy fire, were snuffed out, leaving only voids of black. The gods did not kill him—they considered that mercy—but they cast him down from the highest heaven, through the firmament, to fall for seven days and seven nights. As he plummeted, his armor cracked and broke, his silver mane became tangled with red—the color of his own divine blood, now corrupted by wrath. He crashed into the highest peak of the Wild Zones, creating a crater that would later be called the 'Glory Scar.' He lay there, broken, for a century, the rain washing over him, the snow burying him, the gods' betrayal echoing in his hollow chest. When he finally rose, he was no longer the Unbroken Blade. He was Zartheink the Abandoned, the Great Peril of Wrath.
story.part3: Zartheink's first act upon rising was not to rage against the heavens. It was to gather the fragments of his shattered greatsword and the broken pieces of his armor. With hands that still remembered the craft of creation, he began to shape them—not into a new weapon for himself, but into companions. He infused the shards with his own lingering divine essence and his newfound wrath, and the first Golems of the Glory Scar were born. These small constructs, made of silver metal and crimson stone, were not mindless automatons. They carried a flicker of Zartheink's own loyalty—a loyalty that the gods had scorned but that he would never betray. The golems became his guards, his attendants, his only family. They roamed the mountains, keeping watch, and they would occasionally venture down to protect lost travelers or to drive away those who sought to exploit the Scar's residual divine energy. Zartheink himself remained mostly still, standing like a statue on the peak, his void eyes fixed on the sky. He did not attack mortals. He understood that they were, like him, pawns of the gods. But if any came bearing the symbols of the divine—clerics, paladins, angels—his wrath would ignite. He would speak, his voice a rumble of judgment, demanding to know why they served those who had no loyalty. Those who could not answer were destroyed, their divine blessings stripped away by his mere presence. Those who could—who admitted their own doubts—he sometimes spared, even offering a golem shard as a token of silent kinship.
story.part4: Over the centuries, the legend of the Abandoned Glory spread. The Celestial Order, hearing of a being that could strip divine blessings, labeled Zartheink a Cataclysmic Threat and forbade any from approaching the Glory Scar. They sent inquisitors, but Zartheink's golems turned them back, and his own power was too great for any single champion to overcome. The Order quietly decided to contain rather than confront. The Shadow Accord, on the other hand, saw potential. They sent envoys, offering alliance against the gods. Zartheink listened, then refused. 'I do not seek to destroy the heavens,' he said. 'I seek to make them remember. I want them to see what they threw away, and to know that it was their own fear, not my failure, that broke me.' The envoys left, confused but unharmed. The Forge Pact, fascinated by the golems, attempted to study them from afar. Zartheink allowed it, as long as they did not approach with hostile intent. He even tolerated a few technomancers who came seeking to understand the fusion of divine essence and stone. In them, he saw a reflection of his own creative drive—unappreciated by the powers that be. He shared no secrets, but his silent acceptance spoke volumes. He was not a monster; he was a testament.
story.part5: Now, the aftershocks of the Sundering have reached even the remote Glory Scar. The barrier between the mortal realm and the divine is weakening, and Zartheink feels it—a distant pulse, like the heartbeat of the gods he once served. His void eyes have begun to glow faintly with a deep red light, and his golems have grown more numerous, more active. He senses that the time of reckoning may be approaching. He has started to send his golems to observe certain heroes—those who have been betrayed or abandoned by higher powers: Alucard, whose very blood is a curse from ancient beings; Vash'Tok, cursed by a dead god's relic; Kaal'ian, chained by a former friend; Argus, a fallen angel whose pride was his undoing. Zartheink sees in them kindred spirits, and he wonders if, together, they might form a new pantheon—not of gods, but of the wronged, who would hold the divine accountable. He does not actively recruit; he simply watches, waits, and occasionally leaves a golem shard where a hero might find it—a silent invitation. Zartheink knows that his wrath could consume him, but he also knows that it is the only thing keeping him alive. He will not rest until the gods look down and see him standing, unbroken in spirit, a reminder of their greatest betrayal. And if they refuse to see, then he will climb back to the heavens, one shattered step at a time, and make them see. For he is Zartheink, the Last Faithful, and his faith—though twisted into wrath—will never die.

appearance.faceShape: Ethereal human face, noble and beautiful, high cheekbones, straight nose, full lips, expression of regal sorrow
appearance.hairStyle: Massive lion's mane of thick, wild hair framing the face, flowing down the neck and shoulders
appearance.eyeColor: Absolute black voids, occasionally flickering with deep red light when wrath surges
appearance.facialStructure: Handsome, ageless, with a permanent furrow of pain between the brows
appearance.skinTone: Deep crimson red
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Height of 570 feet; cracked and broken divine silver armor (helm split, breastplate shattered, pauldrons dented); brown veins pulsating visibly across muscles; lion's mane; black void eyes; can summon small golems made of silver shards and red stone

upper.chest: Massive, muscular red chest crisscrossed with brown veins; remnants of a shattered silver breastplate hang loosely, still glowing faintly with divine light
upper.arms: Enormous, corded with muscle, brown veins prominent; silver vambraces, cracked and broken, cling to forearms
upper.shoulders: Broad, with remnants of silver pauldrons, one completely missing, the other hanging by a single strap
upper.neck: Thick, supporting the lion's mane and the broken helm
upper.waist: Narrowed, with a belt of interlocking silver plates, some missing

lower.hips: Covered by a tattered silver war-skirt, shredded at the edges
lower.legs: Pillar-like, red-skinned, with brown veins; silver greaves, heavily damaged, cover the shins
lower.feet: Bare, massive, with thick brown nails

attire.headwear: Cracked silver helm, split down the middle, worn askew; reveals most of the face and the lion's mane
accessories: Fragments of 'Dawn's Oath' (shattered greatsword) embedded in his armor and flesh, occasionally sparking with silver light
Small golems that follow him, constructed of the same silver shards and red stone
attire.distinctFeatures: The broken divine armor is a permanent reminder of his fall; the pulsating brown veins are a sign of his suppressed wrath; the lion's mane moves even without wind; his voice is a deep, resonant hymn

upper.chest: Barrel-chested, immensely muscular
upper.arms: Long, thick, powerful

lower.legs: Pillar-like

weapons.name: Shattered Divinity and Golem Command
weapons.fulldesc: Zartheink wields no intact weapon; his very body and the remnants of his divine armaments are his arsenal. Embedded in his flesh and armor are the shards of 'Dawn's Oath,' his shattered greatsword, which still crackle with silver lightning and divine wrath. He can channel this energy into his fists, delivering blows that sunder both armor and faith. His primary active weapon is the 'Golem Command'—he can summon small constructs made from the silver shards and red stone of the Glory Scar. These golems act as his guards, attacking enemies, intercepting blows, and even self-destructing in bursts of silver fire. The golems are loyal and carry a fragment of Zartheink's own abandoned loyalty. Additionally, his Aura of Righteous Wrath passively weakens divine magic and empowers him when facing multiple foes. His ultimate, 'Glory's Fall,' calls down a rain of shattered celestial metal from the sky, dealing massive area damage and leaving behind a field of golem shards that animate and fight for him.
weapons.look: His fists glow with silver light and crackling red lightning when he strikes. The golems are humanoid, about 20 feet tall, made of silver metal plates and crimson stone, with a single glowing red eye. When 'Glory's Fall' is activated, the sky darkens, and shards of silver metal rain down like a meteor shower, each one embedding in the ground before pulling itself free as a small golem.
weapons.aura: A heavy, oppressive heat of contained fury, the faint sound of distant battle hymns, and a shimmer of silver that tarnishes divine symbols

passive.name: Aura of Righteous Wrath
passive.description: Enemies within 40 meters who bear divine blessings or serve a god (clerics, paladins, angels, etc.) have their healing and damage reduced by 30%, and they take 50 holy damage per second. Zartheink gains a stack of 'Wrath' for each such enemy in the aura (max 10). Each stack increases his physical damage by 5% and reduces damage taken by 2%. Stacks decay after 15 seconds out of combat.

passive.name: Unbroken Will
passive.description: Zartheink is immune to fear, charm, and any effect that would force him to retreat or submit. Whenever he would be stunned or rooted, he instead gains a stack of 'Suppressed Fury'—at 3 stacks, he releases a shockwave that deals 150% physical damage to all enemies within 20 meters and cleanses all crowd control. Additionally, his brown veins pulse visibly when he is below 40% health, increasing his attack speed by 30%.

active.name: Fists of the Abandoned
active.description: Zartheink slams his silver-crackling fists into the ground in a 30-meter cone, dealing 260% physical damage and applying 'Divine Disdain' for 8 seconds. Targets with Divine Disdain take 70 holy damage per second and have their healing received reduced by 60%. If the target is a divine servant, they are also silenced for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Summon Glory Golems
active.description: Zartheink calls forth 3 Glory Golems from the shards around him. Each golem has health equal to 15% of Zartheink's max health, deals 80% physical damage with melee attacks, and will prioritize attacking divine servants. Golems last for 25 seconds or until destroyed. When a golem dies, it explodes in a 10-meter radius, dealing 120% fire/physical damage and leaving a patch of silver shards that slows enemies by 30%. Cooldown: 22 seconds.

ultimate.name: Glory's Fall
ultimate.description: Zartheink raises his arms to the sky and roars, his voice shaking the heavens. For 8 seconds, shards of celestial metal rain down in a 60-meter radius, dealing 150% physical damage per second to all enemies. Each second, 2 Glory Golems rise from the shards embedded in the ground (up to a maximum of 10 active at once). At the end of the duration, all remaining shards explode, dealing 300% physical damage to all enemies in the area. Zartheink is immobile during the channel but gains 80% damage reduction. Cooldown: 150 seconds. Passive: For each golem that survives the duration, Zartheink permanently gains 1% increased golem damage (max 30%).

special.name: Mark of the Forsaken
special.description: When Zartheink reduces an enemy below 10% health, he can choose to 'Mark' them (triggered automatically on next attack unless he chooses otherwise). If the target is a divine servant, they are instantly killed (bosses take 600% physical damage) and Zartheink absorbs their divine essence, healing for 25% of his max health and resetting the cooldown of Summon Glory Golems. If the target is not a divine servant, Zartheink instead spares them, and they receive a 'Shard of the Abandoned'—a permanent item that grants resistance to divine magic but also marks them as an ally of Zartheink, causing some divine beings to be hostile.

ratings.notes: A Cataclysmic Threat that specializes in anti-divine warfare and construct summoning. His presence is devastating to clerics, paladins, and any who wield divine power. He is not hostile to those who do not serve the gods, and a non-combat approach is possible if players demonstrate that they have also been wronged by higher powers. He is slow but hits incredibly hard, and his golems can overwhelm unprepared groups. Vulnerability: Calming effects (sleep, pacify) can temporarily reduce his Wrath stacks, but may also trigger a more dangerous phase. Holy damage deals bonus damage to him but also enrages him, increasing his attack speed.

skills.name: Aura of Righteous Wrath
skills.description: 40m aura: Divine servants take -30% healing/dmg, 50 holy/sec. Zartheink gains Wrath stacks (+5% dmg, -2% dmg taken per stack, max 10).

skills.name: Fists of the Abandoned
skills.description: 30m cone, 260% phys dmg, Divine Disdain (70 holy/sec, -60% healing) for 8s. Silence divine servants 3s. CD: 10s.

skills.name: Summon Glory Golems
skills.description: Summon 3 golems (15% health each, 80% dmg). Last 25s. Explode on death: 120% fire/phys dmg, slow 30%. CD: 22s.

skills.name: Glory's Fall
skills.description: 60m radius, 8s channel (80% DR). 150% phys/sec, spawn 2 golems/sec (max 10). End explosion 300% phys. Permanent +1% golem dmg per surviving golem (max 30%). CD: 150s.

monsters.lore: Zartheink was the Seraph of Glory, the divine champion who led the gods' armies to victory against chaos. For his faithful service, the gods rewarded him with betrayal—they cast him down, stripped his rank, and shattered his armor, fearing his growing power. He crashed into the Wild Zones, his eyes turned to void, his heart filled with a cold, focused wrath directed only at the divine. He does not harm mortals unless they serve the gods who wronged him. From the shards of his broken greatsword, he crafted loyal golems to guard his solitude. He stands as a monument to divine ingratitude, waiting for the day the heavens remember what they threw away.

relationships.description: Both were divine beings cast down by the gods. Zartheink sees in Argus a reflection of his own fall, though Argus's was due to pride, while Zartheink's was due to loyalty. He has sent a golem to observe Argus, and if they meet, Zartheink might offer a silent alliance against the divine.

Kaal'ian was cursed by a fellow god for his compassion. Zartheink was cast down for his service. They share a bond of divine betrayal, and Zartheink's golems have been seen near the Chained Sanctum, as if offering silent solidarity.
Vash'Tok's curse originates from a dead god's relic. Zartheink sees him as another victim of divine caprice and would likely offer aid if Vash'Tok sought to break his curse.
The Celestial Order represents the very gods who betrayed him. Zartheink will attack any member on sight, stripping their blessings before crushing them. He does not negotiate with them.

monsters.specialty: Anti-divine raid boss with strong summoning mechanics. His Aura of Righteous Wrath makes him especially dangerous to clerics, paladins, and any class relying on divine magic. The encounter can be approached non-violently if players do not bear divine symbols and can convince him they share his grievance. His golems must be managed carefully, as they can quickly overwhelm. Holy damage dealers should be cautious, as their attacks both hurt and enrage him. Calming spells can temporarily reduce his damage output but risk triggering a 'Suppressed Fury' burst.

dialogue.selection: Do you serve them? The ones who cast me down?
dialogue.attack: FEEL THE WRATH OF THE ABANDONED!
dialogue.ultimate: GLORY'S FALL! WITNESS WHAT YOU THREW AWAY!
dialogue.death: Finally... I will see... if they remember...

monsters.name: Wolfazar O Helm
titles: He Who Binds with Iron Will
monsters.shortDescription: Once a benevolent king who ruled with justice and love, Wolfazar O Helm was betrayed by his own blood brother, leading to the slaughter of his people. In his dying breath, he forged a pact with the demon god Figxang, trading his soul and his subjects' humanity for resurrection. Now he rises as a monstrous tyrant—a wolf‑headed, lion‑limbed colossus clad in royal demonic armor, his majestic helm concealing all but his piercing sapphire eyes. He rules his damned kingdom with absolute authority, his will enforced by the very people he saved, now transformed into the Damned Legion. He does not seek conquest; he seeks to prevent betrayal by crushing any hint of disobedience. To stand before Wolfazar is to face the weight of a broken oath and the cold, unyielding tyranny born from a love that was repaid with treachery.
monsters.fullDescription: The throne room of the Cursed Citadel does not echo with celebration—it hums with the silent, oppressive weight of absolute rule. At its heart, upon a dais of black iron, stands Wolfazar O Helm, a towering figure of monstrous majesty and profound sorrow. He is a fusion of king and beast: a humanoid body of immense muscular perfection, his fair skin stretched taut over biceps and abdominals that speak of a warrior's discipline, yet his limbs are those of a great lion—powerful, clawed, and covered in fine white fur. A thick, voluminous mane of white and silver cascades from his neck and shoulders, merging with the fur of his chest. His head is that of an alpha wolf, but it is encased in a helm of sublime, terrible beauty. This helm, forged from demonic silver and accented with royal purple and blood‑red, covers his entire face, leaving only two slits through which burn eyes of flawless sapphire blue—windows to a soul trapped between a king's compassion and a tyrant's resolve. Majestic horns, curved like a ram's but gleaming with an inner purple light, sweep back from the helm, adding to his regal menace. His armor is minimal yet imposing: a sculpted breastplate of black iron with purple runes, spiked pauldrons, and a tattered royal cape of deep red that drags behind him. He wields no weapon; his very presence is a weapon—his voice a decree that can shatter wills, his gaze a command that brooks no defiance. Around him, shadows move—his Damned Legion, the resurrected people he once loved, now wolf‑lion hybrids who serve with a loyalty born of both gratitude and eternal fear. Wolfazar does not rage; he rules. And his rule is absolute, for he knows that mercy led to betrayal, and only iron can prevent it from happening again.

story.part1: Long before the Sundering scarred the skies, the Kingdom of Helm was a beacon of prosperity and justice in the heart of Aethelgard. Its king, Wolfazar, was beloved by all—a wise ruler who walked among his people, who listened to their woes, and who ruled not with fear but with a gentle, guiding hand. He had a twin brother, Valdemar, with whom he had shared every childhood joy and every kingly burden. They were inseparable, and Wolfazar trusted Valdemar with his life and his kingdom. But envy is a poison that festers in the shadows. Valdemar, always the second son, the lesser twin, grew to resent Wolfazar's popularity and power. He began to whisper with envious nobles and to parley with the kingdom's enemies in secret. Wolfazar, blinded by love and trust, saw nothing. The day of the Harvest Festival, when the kingdom's gates were thrown open to all, Valdemar's plan unfolded. Foreign mercenaries, bribed with promises of plunder, flooded the capital. The royal guard was overwhelmed. Wolfazar, caught in the chaos, fought desperately to protect his people, but it was too late. The city burned, and the streets ran red with the blood of the innocent. Valdemar, standing on the palace balcony, watched with cold satisfaction. Wolfazar confronted his brother, demanding to know why. 'Because you had everything,' Valdemar sneered, 'and I had nothing but your shadow.' In that moment, Wolfazar's heart shattered. Before he could strike, a mercenary's arrow pierced his chest. He fell, his vision fading, the screams of his dying people filling his ears.
story.part2: As Wolfazar lay dying amid the carnage of his beloved kingdom, his spirit cried out—not for revenge, but for salvation. He begged any power, any force, to save his people, to undo the horror that his trust had allowed. The heavens were silent, but the Abyss answered. A voice, smooth as poisoned honey and deep as the grave, spoke into his mind. It was Figxang, the Demon God of Dominion, a being who thrived on oaths and the iron bonds of absolute rule. 'I can give you the power to resurrect your people,' Figxang whispered. 'But there is a price. Your soul will be mine, and your people will return not as they were, but as reflections of your new nature—creatures of order and ferocity, bound to your will forever. You will be their king, their tyrant, and you will never trust again.' Wolfazar, with his last breath, accepted. Darkness swallowed him. When he awoke, he was no longer in the ruined city but in a desolate plain that would become the Wild Zones. His body had been transformed: he was larger, monstrously muscular, his limbs those of a lion, his head that of a wolf. And upon his face was a helm of demonic silver, sealed tight, hiding the human visage he had lost. Around him, rising from the blood‑soaked earth, were his people—resurrected, but changed. They were now wolf‑lion hybrids, their eyes glowing with a faint purple light, their forms powerful and bestial. They looked upon Wolfazar with a mixture of awe and terror, and they knelt. Wolfazar, the Damned King, had been born.
story.part3: The Kingdom of Helm was rebuilt, but it was no longer a beacon of justice. It became the Cursed Citadel, a fortress of black iron and purple flame, hidden deep within the Wild Zones. Wolfazar ruled with an iron fist, his word absolute law. The Damned Legion, his resurrected people, served him without question—they owed him their existence, and they remembered the horror of their deaths. They policed the land with savage efficiency, crushing any dissent, any hint of betrayal, before it could take root. Wolfazar did not enjoy cruelty; he simply saw no alternative. His brother Valdemar had taught him that trust was weakness, and he would not be weak again. He issued decrees that controlled every aspect of life: trade, travel, even speech was regulated. Those who obeyed lived in relative peace; those who questioned or defied were dealt with swiftly, their fate a warning to others. Over the centuries, the legend of the Wolf‑Lion Tyrant spread. Adventurers who ventured into his domain were given a choice: kneel and serve, or leave and never return. Those who attacked were torn apart by the Legion. Wolfazar never left his citadel; his sapphire eyes watched over his domain from his throne, ever vigilant. He sometimes gazed into a shard of polished obsidian that showed him the face he had lost—the kind king he once was—and a single tear of purple light would fall from beneath his helm. But he never wavered. Tyranny was his burden, his penance, and his only way to protect what remained of his people.
story.part4: The demon god Figxang visited Wolfazar only once after the pact. He appeared as a shadow with a thousand eyes, and he spoke with amusement. 'You have done well, my king. Your iron rule feeds my power. But remember, your soul is mine. When you finally fall, you will serve me in the Abyss.' Wolfazar, his sapphire eyes unblinking, replied, 'I know. But as long as I rule, my people are safe from betrayal. That is enough.' Figxang laughed and departed. Wolfazar understood that his tyranny was a cage—not just for his subjects, but for himself. He could never again experience trust, friendship, or love. He had traded his humanity for the survival of his people, and he would bear that cost until the end of eternity. Over time, some of his Damned Legion began to show signs of independent thought—flickers of the people they once were. They would sometimes gaze at the king with something other than fear: a lingering sorrow, a memory of the benevolent ruler he had been. Wolfazar noticed, but he did not encourage it. He feared that if his people remembered too much, they might begin to question his rule, and then he would have to crush them as he crushed all dissent. It was a cruel irony: the very love that had driven him to save them now forced him to suppress their humanity.
story.part5: Now, the aftershocks of the Sundering have weakened the barriers that hid the Cursed Citadel. The Forge Pact, ever hungry for new technology, has detected the unique demonic energy emanating from Wolfazar's domain. The Celestial Order sees a corrupted king and his monstrous legion as a threat to divine order. And the Shadow Accord, intrigued by Figxang's involvement, wishes to study—or recruit—the Damned King. Wolfazar, aware of these stirrings, has not mobilized his forces for war. He waits, watching with his sapphire eyes. He knows that heroes will come—some seeking to destroy him, others seeking to free his people, and perhaps, just perhaps, one who understands the weight of a necessary tyranny. He has heard whispers of other tragic figures: Minsitthar, a king betrayed by his own brother; Argus, a fallen angel who rebelled against divine authority; Zartheink, abandoned by the gods he served. Wolfazar feels a kinship with them, though he has never met them. He wonders if, together, the betrayed and the damned might forge a new kind of order—one not based on fear, but on a shared understanding of pain. But his pact with Figxang binds him, and his own heart is encased in iron. Until someone can prove to him that trust is not always a weakness, Wolfazar will remain the Great Peril of Tyranny, a king who rules with an iron will, his sapphire eyes forever watching, forever mourning, forever vigilant against the betrayal that broke him.

appearance.faceShape: Alpha wolf head, broad muzzle, sharp ears; entirely concealed beneath a majestic demonic helm
appearance.nose: Wolf snout, black and wet, visible only in shadow beneath the helm
appearance.hairStyle: Thick, voluminous white and silver mane that cascades from the head and neck, merging with chest fur; body fur is white on lion limbs
appearance.eyeColor: Sapphire Blue, glowing with inner light, visible through helm slits
appearance.facialStructure: Wolf-like, but hidden; the helm gives an impression of regal, predatory majesty
appearance.skinTone: Fair human skin on torso, arms, and face (where not covered by fur or helm)
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Majestic curved horns of demonic purple and silver protruding from helm; royal demonic armor (minimal: sculpted breastplate, spiked pauldrons, tattered red cape); lion-like legs and hands with white fur and retractable claws; helm covers entire face except for sapphire eyes; aura of oppressive authority

upper.chest: Barrel-chested, fair human skin with sculpted abdominal muscles; minimal black iron breastplate with glowing purple runes, leaving much of the chest exposed
upper.arms: Humanoid upper arms, fair-skinned and muscular; forearms and hands are leonine, covered in white fur, with retractable claws
upper.shoulders: Broad, adorned with spiked pauldrons of black iron and purple accents
upper.neck: Thick, wrapped in white mane fur
upper.waist: Narrow, with a belt of black iron chains and a purple gemstone buckle

lower.hips: Covered by a tattered red royal cape that drags behind; beneath, a leather and iron skirt
lower.legs: Leonine, digitigrade, covered in white fur, powerfully muscled
lower.feet: Large lion paws with white fur and black claws
