Black iron chains around waist and wrists
Purple gemstone on belt (focus of Figxang's power)
attire.distinctFeatures: The helm is permanently sealed; Wolfazar cannot remove it. The sapphire eyes are the only visible human feature. The mane is thick and voluminous, giving him an alpha presence. The lion limbs are covered in fine white fur.

upper.chest: Incredibly muscular, defined abs and pecs
upper.arms: Biceps and triceps bulging; forearms leonine

lower.legs: Powerful, digitigrade, covered in fur

weapons.name: Iron Will and Command of the Damned Legion
weapons.fulldesc: Wolfazar wields no blade; his weapons are his unyielding will and the loyalty of his transformed people. His presence alone projects an Aura of Absolute Rule that saps the resolve of enemies, reducing their damage and forcing them to kneel if they attempt to defy him. He can issue Decrees—verbal commands that carry magical force—silencing spellcasters, rooting warriors in place, or compelling obedience. His physical strikes, delivered with his leonine claws, are devastating, causing deep bleeding wounds that fester with the memory of betrayal (reducing healing). His most feared ability is 'Command of the Damned Legion': he summons spectral or physical manifestations of his wolf‑lion subjects to fight alongside him, swarming enemies and dragging them down. His ultimate, 'Tyrant's Decree,' creates a domain where his word is law—enemies within cannot use movement abilities, are slowed, and if they attack Wolfazar, they are forced to kneel (stunned) as their own will is crushed. Wolfazar does not fight with rage; he fights with the cold certainty of a king who has already judged his foes and found them wanting.
weapons.look: When issuing a Decree, his sapphire eyes flare brightly and his voice reverberates with unnatural authority. His claws leave trails of purple energy. The Damned Legionnaires appear as ghostly wolf‑lion hybrids or as solid, armored warriors wielding spectral blades. The Tyrant's Decree manifests as a visible purple and red dome of oppressive energy, inside which all sound becomes muffled and movements feel heavy.
weapons.aura: A crushing weight of authority, the faint sound of a royal decree being read, and a purple‑tinged haze that makes enemies feel small and powerless

passive.name: Aura of Absolute Rule
passive.description: Enemies within 35 meters of Wolfazar have their damage dealt reduced by 20% and their movement speed reduced by 15%. Additionally, any enemy that attacks Wolfazar gains a stack of 'Defiance' (max 3). At 3 stacks, the enemy is 'Cowed' for 5 seconds: they are silenced and cannot attack Wolfazar directly. This effect has a 40-second cooldown per target.

passive.name: The Damned King's Vigil
passive.description: Wolfazar is immune to fear, charm, and any effect that would cause him to lose control of his actions. When he falls below 50% health, his Aura of Absolute Rule expands to 50 meters and its damage reduction increases to 30%. Additionally, his sapphire eyes weep tears of purple light, and all his Decree abilities have their cooldowns reduced by 30% for the remainder of the battle.

active.name: King's Decree: Kneel
active.description: Wolfazar points at a target within 40 meters and utters a single word. The target must make a willpower check or be forced to kneel (rooted and silenced) for 4 seconds. While kneeling, they take 60 psychic damage per second. If the target is already Cowed, the effect is automatic and lasts 6 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

active.name: Claws of the Betrayed
active.description: Wolfazar slashes with his leonine claws in a 20-meter cone, dealing 210% physical damage and applying 'Sovereign's Bleed' for 10 seconds. Sovereign's Bleed deals 40 physical damage per second and reduces healing received by 50%. Enemies affected by Sovereign's Bleed also leave a trail of blood that slows other enemies who walk through it by 20%. Cooldown: 9 seconds.

ultimate.name: Tyrant's Decree
ultimate.description: Wolfazar raises his hand, and a 55-meter radius dome of oppressive purple and red energy descends, lasting for 10 seconds. Inside the dome: enemies are slowed by 50%, cannot use any movement abilities (dashes, blinks, teleports), and take 80 psychic damage per second. Any enemy that attacks Wolfazar while inside the dome is instantly forced to kneel (stunned) for 3 seconds. Additionally, Wolfazar summons 4 Damned Legionnaires (spectral wolf‑lion warriors) that fight for him, each with 20% of his health and dealing 70% physical damage. Cooldown: 130 seconds. Passive: For each enemy that kneels inside Tyrant's Decree, Wolfazar permanently gains 1% increased damage with his Decree abilities (max 25%).

special.name: Legion's Oath
special.description: When Wolfazar reduces an enemy below 10% health, he can command them to 'Swear Fealty' (triggered automatically on next attack unless he chooses otherwise). The target must make a willpower check or be instantly dominated (non‑boss): they become an ally for 30 seconds, gaining 50% increased damage and damage reduction, after which they are released. Bosses instead take 500% physical damage and are silenced for 5 seconds. If the target was Cowed, the domination is automatic. A successful domination also heals Wolfazar for 15% of his max health and summons a free Damned Legionnaire.

ratings.notes: A Cataclysmic Threat that specializes in control and debilitation. His Aura of Absolute Rule and Decree abilities make him a nightmare for melee attackers and spellcasters alike. His summoned Legionnaires can overwhelm, and his ultimate turns the battlefield into a prison. He is not inherently evil; his tyranny stems from a desire to protect, and players who can demonstrate genuine, selfless loyalty (e.g., by refusing to attack him even when provoked, or by speaking of their own betrayed trusts) may trigger a unique dialogue and potentially a non‑violent resolution. He is vulnerable to fire (remnant of his kingdom's burning) and to light/holy damage, which reminds him of his lost humanity and can temporarily stun him.

skills.name: Aura of Absolute Rule
skills.description: 35m aura: -20% damage dealt, -15% move speed. Attacking Wolfazar grants Defiance stacks; 3 stacks = Cowed (silence, cannot attack Wolfazar) for 5s.

skills.name: King's Decree: Kneel
skills.description: 40m range, single target: willpower check or kneel (rooted, silenced) 4s, 60 psychic/sec. Auto on Cowed targets (6s). CD: 12s.

skills.name: Claws of the Betrayed
skills.description: 20m cone, 210% phys dmg, Sovereign's Bleed (40 phys/sec, -50% healing) for 10s. Blood trail slows enemies 20%. CD: 9s.

skills.name: Tyrant's Decree
skills.description: 55m dome, 10s: -50% speed, no movement abilities, 80 psychic/sec. Attacking Wolfazar = kneel 3s. Summons 4 Legionnaires. Permanent +1% Decree dmg per kneeling enemy (max 25%). CD: 130s.

monsters.lore: Wolfazar O Helm was once a benevolent king, beloved by his people, until his twin brother betrayed him, leading to the massacre of his kingdom. Dying, he forged a pact with the demon god Figxang, trading his soul and his people's humanity for resurrection. He rose as a monstrous wolf‑lion tyrant, his helm forever hiding his face, his sapphire eyes the only remnant of his former self. He rules his Damned Legion with absolute authority, crushing any hint of betrayal. He does not relish his tyranny; he sees it as the only way to protect what remains. Deep within, the kind king still mourns, waiting for a soul who can prove that trust is not always a weakness.

relationships.description: Both were kings betrayed by those closest to them. Wolfazar has heard tales of Minsitthar and feels a deep, unspoken connection. If they met, Wolfazar would see a reflection of his own pain, and perhaps a path toward something other than tyranny.

Argus fell due to pride; Wolfazar fell due to love. Both now rule over domains of their own making. Wolfazar respects Argus's power but sees his arrogance as a flaw that could lead to further betrayal.

Zartheink was cast down by the gods; Wolfazar was saved by a demon. They share a sense of divine injustice. Wolfazar's golems (or Legionnaires) have been observed near the Glory Scar, as if offering a silent alliance of the forsaken.

The Celestial Order represents the kind of absolute, unquestioning authority that Wolfazar despises—a rule without understanding. He sees them as potential tyrants themselves and will not suffer their presence.

monsters.specialty: Control‑oriented raid boss with strong debuffs and summoned minions. His abilities punish aggression and force careful positioning. The encounter can be approached diplomatically if players demonstrate genuine loyalty (e.g., by not attacking first, by sharing stories of their own betrayals). If trust is established, Wolfazar may offer a boon or even become a temporary ally against a greater threat. In combat, focus on kiting his Legionnaires, cleansing bleeds, and avoiding Defiance stacks. Fire and holy damage are effective but may trigger enrage or stun phases.

dialogue.selection: You stand before the Iron Crown. State your purpose.
dialogue.ultimate: TYRANT'S DECREE! LET ALL WITNESS THE PRICE OF DEFIANCE!
dialogue.death: My people... I have failed you... again...

monsters.name: Aischrostengz
monsters.shortDescription: Born in the scented shadows of a brothel, Aischrostengz was a human woman who learned early that desire and shame are two sides of the same coin, minted by the hypocritical gods who judge what they secretly crave. When a minor deity of purity attempted to curse her with the touch of Lust, she did not break—she bloomed. Now she walks the Wild Zones as a being of terrible beauty: long white hair cascading over flawless human features, eyes of piercing red iris, and magnificent crimson wings that are as beautiful as they are unashamed. She wears her past openly, her revealing noble attire a declaration that the body is not sin. She is kind to mortals, protective of the vulnerable, and utterly merciless to gods and their sycophantic followers. To face Aischrostengz is to have your every hidden shame laid bare, your hypocrisy exposed, and your soul weighed not by divine law, but by the simple, damning truth of who you truly are.
monsters.fullDescription: The Wild Zones are a place of untamed chaos, but when Aischrostengz steps from the shadows, a strange stillness falls—not of fear, but of anticipation. She is not a towering monster; she stands at a mortal woman's height, her form graceful and perfectly proportioned, her skin a warm human tone that seems to glow softly in dim light. Her face is a masterpiece of delicate features: high cheekbones, a straight nose, full lips that curve in a knowing smile, and eyes of deep crimson iris that hold both ancient sorrow and fierce defiance. Her hair is a cascade of long, voluminous white, layered and flowing like moonlit water, framing her face and spilling over her shoulders. From her back sprout two large wings, not the leathery pinions of a demon, but flawless, feathered wings of vivid red—the color of passion, of lifeblood, of unapologetic desire. They are beautiful, preened, and they move with a gentle rustle that speaks of grace, not menace. She wears minimal clothing: a cropped top that bares her midriff, a pair of fitted shorts, and elegant heeled shoes. Her arms are draped with shawl-like sleeves of fine, translucent fabric that whisper of nobility even in their scantiness. Every inch of her presentation is deliberate—she is not hiding, not apologizing. She is the Great Peril of Shame, not because she feels it, but because she reflects it back upon a world that shames desire, that shames the body, that shames the very essence of being alive. When she speaks, her voice is a warm contralto, laced with irony and compassion. She moves with the confidence of one who has nothing left to hide, and her presence strips away the masks of all who meet her gaze.

story.part1: The brothel called 'The Velvet Hush' was not a place of degradation, but a sanctuary for women who had few other choices in the rigid, god-fearing society of Aethelgard. There, in a room scented with jasmine and old incense, a child was born to a courtesan named Lysara. She named her daughter Aischrostengz—an old, forgotten name meaning 'unbroken thread.' The girl grew up surrounded by the laughter and tears of women who sold their bodies but guarded their souls. She learned to read the desires of men, to see the hypocrisy of the pious merchants and clerics who visited by night, preaching purity by day. She never judged her mother or her sisters; she saw them as survivors in a world built to exploit them. When she was seventeen, a minor deity of Purity, a vain and petty being named Caelor, took offense at the brothel's existence. He decided to make an example. Descending in a shaft of cold light, he declared that the women were vessels of sin and that he would cleanse them. He reached for Aischrostengz, the youngest, and attempted to 'bless' her with the touch of Lust—a curse that would turn her into a succubus, a creature of insatiable desire, forever marked as shameful. But Aischrostengz did not scream or beg. She looked into the god's eyes and saw his own hidden lust, his own fear of desire. She reached out and, in a moment of defiance that echoed through the divine realms, she did not reject the curse—she accepted it, but on her own terms. The power flooded into her, but instead of twisting her into a demon, it transformed her wings to crimson and her eyes to red, while her heart remained human. Caelor, stunned and humiliated by his own failed judgment, fled. Aischrostengz stood in the ruined courtyard, her new wings spreading, and she laughed—a sound of pure, liberated joy.
story.part2: The transformation brought with it strange powers. Aischrostengz could now see the hidden shames of others—the secret vices, the unconfessed desires, the petty cruelties they hid behind pious masks. She could make these shames manifest, not as illusions but as palpable truths that others could perceive. She used this gift first to protect The Velvet Hush. When a corrupt magistrate tried to shut down the brothel to cover his own indiscretions, Aischrostengz simply stood before him and let him see himself as others saw him—a lecherous, hypocritical coward. He fled the city in disgrace. Word spread of the 'Red-Winged Woman' who could unveil any secret. The downtrodden and the shamed began to seek her out, and she offered them not judgment but understanding. She told them that their desires were not sins, that their bodies were not shameful, and that the gods who preached purity were the most impure of all. She became a folk hero to the marginalized, a whisper of hope in the shadow of the cathedrals. The Celestial Order, hearing of a being who defied a god and wielded the power of shame against the righteous, labeled her a dangerous anomaly. But they could not easily move against her, for she had committed no crime—she simply revealed what was already there. And many in the Order feared what she might reveal about them.
story.part3: Aischrostengz did not remain in the city. She knew that her presence would eventually draw a more violent response from the divine. She bid farewell to her mother and sisters, leaving them with a blessing of protection—a crimson feather that would warn them of danger. She walked into the Wild Zones, a realm where outcasts and monsters roamed, and she found a kind of peace there. She discovered that her powers were not limited to humans; even beasts and lesser demons carried their own forms of shame—fear, weakness, unfulfilled instincts. She could calm a raging manticore by showing it that its fury stemmed from loneliness, or she could drive a demon to madness by reflecting its own self-loathing. She built no fortress, but she became known as the 'Brothel Saint,' a paradoxical figure of comfort and terror. Travelers lost in the Wild Zones sometimes encountered her—a beautiful woman with red wings sitting by a campfire. She would offer them food and conversation, and if they were honest, she would guide them safely. If they were liars or predators, she would show them the truth of their own souls, and they would flee screaming into the night. She never killed unless absolutely necessary; she believed that shame, truly confronted, could lead to change. But for gods and their chosen, she had no mercy.
story.part4: The gods did not forget Aischrostengz. Caelor, humiliated, whispered to his fellow deities that she was a threat to divine order. A few attempted to confront her indirectly—sending divine beasts or blessed champions. Each time, Aischrostengz turned their own hidden shames against them. A champion of the light, sent to slay her, was revealed to have murdered his own brother in a fit of jealousy; he fell to his knees and wept, his holy sword clattering uselessly. A celestial hound, bred to hunt demons, was shown the loneliness of its kennel and the cruelty of its masters; it turned and fled back to the heavens, howling. The gods, frustrated, debated whether to smite her directly. But a direct divine intervention would be an admission that a mortal-born woman had bested them, and their pride would not allow it. So they did what gods often do: they ignored her, hoping she would fade into legend. Aischrostengz, however, did not fade. She continued to wander, her red wings a beacon to those who had been shamed by the world. She became a symbol of resistance against divine hypocrisy, a living reminder that the truth, however uncomfortable, will always outlast lies.
story.part5: The Sundering's aftershocks have stirred the old powers, and the gods are once again active in the affairs of Kretos. Aischrostengz senses this shift—a cold wind from the celestial realms, carrying the scent of ancient judgment. She knows that the time for quiet wandering may be ending. She has begun to seek out others who have been wronged by the divine: Argus, the fallen angel; Kaal'ian, the chained seraph; Zartheink, the abandoned glory. She believes that together, they might form a new covenant—not to destroy the gods, but to hold them accountable, to demand that they face their own shame. She is also aware of the other Great Perils, including Pixley, the Peril of Lust. The two share a connection, though Aischrostengz views Pixley's untamed, consuming desire with a mixture of pity and wariness. She wonders if even a being of pure lust might be freed from its own nature. For now, Aischrostengz continues her solitary path, her crimson wings carrying her across the Wild Zones. She aids the lost, shames the wicked, and waits. She knows that soon, heroes will come—some seeking her blessing, others seeking to destroy her. She will meet them with the same unwavering gaze, the same unashamed truth. And if the gods themselves finally descend to silence her, she will stand before them and ask only one question: 'What are you so afraid that I will see?'

appearance.faceShape: Graceful, delicate, with high cheekbones, a straight nose, and full lips; eternally calm and knowing expression
appearance.hairStyle: Long, voluminous, layered white hair cascading down back and shoulders
appearance.eyeColor: Crimson Red (iris), human sclera
appearance.facialStructure: Flawless, beautiful, human; no monstrous features
appearance.skinTone: Warm human skin tone, slightly luminous
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Large, flawless feathered wings of vivid red; eyes glow faintly when using her powers; wears minimal revealing attire (cropped top, shorts, heeled shoes, shawl-like sleeves) that appears both noble and unashamed; no visible weapons

upper.chest: Cropped top of fine grey fabric, trimmed with black lace, leaving midriff exposed; elegant and revealing but not vulgar
upper.arms: Shawl-like sleeves of translucent grey silk draped from shoulders to wrists, fluttering with movement

lower.hips: Fitted black shorts, modest but form-fitting
lower.legs: Long, shapely, bare
lower.feet: Heeled shoes of black leather with delicate ankle straps

attire.headwear: None; her white hair is her crown
accessories: A single crimson feather tucked behind her ear (a token from her mother)
attire.distinctFeatures: The red wings are the most striking feature—beautiful, feathered, and completely unashamed. Her attire is revealing but conveys confidence and nobility, not seduction. Her eyes hold a perpetual, knowing calm.

monsters.armorType: None (Relies on Agility and Psychic Defense)

upper.chest: Full, proportionate, fit
upper.arms: Slender but toned

lower.legs: Long, athletic

weapons.name: Gaze of Unveiling and Crimson Pinions
weapons.fulldesc: Aischrostengz wields no steel; her weapons are truth and desire. Her primary ability is the 'Gaze of Unveiling'—by meeting a target's eyes, she can perceive their deepest, most hidden shame (a secret sin, a repressed desire, a moment of cowardice) and force them to confront it. The psychic shock can stun, debilitate, or even break the will of those who are unable to accept their own truth. She can also project this shame outward, making it visible to others as a spectral aura that marks the target and reduces their standing with allies. Her crimson wings are not just for flight; she can buffet enemies with gusts of shame-laden wind, or she can use her feathers as projectiles that carry the weight of exposed secrets. Her ultimate ability, 'Shame's Revelation,' creates a zone where all creatures are stripped of their pretenses—debuffs are shared, hidden weaknesses become visible, and those who have committed acts of cruelty or hypocrisy take damage proportional to their misdeeds. Aischrostengz does not enjoy causing pain; she uses these abilities only against those who would harm the innocent or who serve hypocritical gods.
weapons.look: When using Gaze of Unveiling, her crimson eyes glow brightly and the target may see fleeting visions of their own past. Her wing attacks leave trails of red light. Shame's Revelation manifests as a grey-black haze that makes everything appear stark and unflattering, with whispered confessions echoing faintly.
weapons.aura: A subtle feeling of exposure, as if one's secrets are written on the skin; a faint scent of jasmine and old incense

passive.name: Aura of Exposed Secrets
passive.description: Enemies within 30 meters of Aischrostengz have their critical hit chance reduced by 25% and their damage dealt reduced by 10% (non-stacking with similar effects). Additionally, any stealth or invisibility is automatically broken within the aura, and hidden traps or illusions are revealed. Allies within the aura gain 15% increased critical hit chance, as they feel emboldened by the lack of deception.

passive.name: Unashamed Heart
passive.description: Aischrostengz is immune to fear, charm, and any effect that would alter her perception of self. When she takes damage from a divine source (god, angel, blessed weapon), she reflects 30% of that damage back as psychic damage and gains a stack of 'Righteous Defiance' (max 5). Each stack increases her psychic damage by 5% and reduces damage taken from divine sources by 5%.

active.name: Gaze of Unveiling
active.description: Aischrostengz locks eyes with a single target within 40 meters. The target must make a willpower check or be 'Shamed' for 8 seconds. While Shamed, the target takes 70 psychic damage per second, is silenced, and has their damage dealt reduced by 30%. Additionally, any allies of the target within 20 meters also see glimpses of the shame, causing them to deal 10% less damage to Aischrostengz. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

active.name: Crimson Pinions
active.description: Aischrostengz beats her wings, creating a 25-meter cone of red-tinged wind. Enemies in the cone take 160% physical damage and are knocked back 10 meters. They also are afflicted with 'Exposed' for 6 seconds, increasing damage taken from all sources by 20%. If an enemy is already Shamed, the knockback distance is doubled and they are stunned for 1 second upon landing. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: Shame's Revelation
ultimate.description: Aischrostengz spreads her wings wide and releases a pulse of pure, undeniable truth in a 50-meter radius for 8 seconds. Inside the zone: all enemies lose any active beneficial buffs (including stealth, shields, damage enhancements); they are 'Stripped' and cannot gain new buffs. Additionally, any enemy that has dealt damage to an ally or innocent creature in the last minute takes 200% psychic damage per second (capped at 800% total). Aischrostengz is immobile for the initial 2-second cast but gains 70% damage reduction. Cooldown: 140 seconds. Passive: For each enemy that dies while Stripped, Aischrostengz permanently gains 1% increased duration of her Shame effects (max 20%).

special.name: Confession's Release
special.description: When Aischrostengz reduces an enemy below 10% health, she can offer them 'Release' (triggered automatically unless she chooses otherwise). The target must make a willpower check or confess their deepest shame aloud. If they confess, they are healed for 30% of their max health, all debuffs are removed, and they become neutral to Aischrostengz for 1 hour (non-boss). If they refuse or are a boss, they take 500% psychic damage and are silenced for 8 seconds. A successful confession also restores 20% of Aischrostengz's mana and reduces the cooldown of Gaze of Unveiling by 50%.

ratings.notes: A Cataclysmic Threat that specializes in psychic warfare, debuff stripping, and anti-divine mechanics. She is fragile if caught in melee, but her high speed, flight, and powerful crowd control make her extremely dangerous. Her abilities are particularly devastating against divine casters and self-righteous paladins. She is not aggressive by nature; she will parley with those who approach honestly. Her weakness is pure physical damage and attacks that cannot be shamed (constructs, true undead).

skills.name: Aura of Exposed Secrets
skills.description: 30m aura: -25% enemy crit chance, -10% damage dealt. Reveals stealth/traps. Allies +15% crit chance.

skills.name: Gaze of Unveiling
skills.description: 40m range, single target: willpower check or Shamed 8s (70 psychic/sec, silenced, -30% dmg). Allies of target deal -10% to Aischrostengz. CD: 12s.

skills.name: Crimson Pinions
skills.description: 25m cone, 160% phys dmg, knockback 10m, Exposed 6s (+20% dmg taken). Double knockback + stun if Shamed. CD: 10s.

skills.name: Shame's Revelation
skills.description: 50m radius, 8s channel (70% DR). Strip all buffs, prevent new buffs. Enemies who harmed allies/innocents take 200% psychic/sec (max 800%). Permanent +1% Shame duration per kill (max 20%). CD: 140s.

monsters.lore: Aischrostengz was born in a brothel, raised by women who taught her that shame is a weapon of the powerful. When a god tried to curse her with Lust, she accepted it on her own terms, transforming into a being of red wings and crimson eyes, still human at heart. She now wanders the Wild Zones, using her power to unveil hidden truths and expose hypocrisy. She is kind to mortals, protective of the shamed, and utterly merciless to gods and their followers. She is the Great Peril of Shame, not because she feels it, but because she reflects it back upon a world that uses shame to control.

relationships.description: Argus rebelled against the gods out of pride; Aischrostengz defies them out of truth. She respects his power but sees his arrogance as a flaw. She might offer him a mirror to see himself truly.
Both were cursed by gods for their compassion. Aischrostengz has visited the Chained Sanctum in spirit, offering silent solidarity. She believes Kaal'ian's endurance is a form of unashamed truth.

Pixley is the Great Peril of Lust, a being of pure, untamed desire. Aischrostengz is Lust-touched but retains her humanity. She pities Pixley's endless hunger and wonders if even a Peril can be freed from its nature.
Alucard is a human cursed by his bloodline, fighting his own darkness. Aischrostengz sees him as a brother in the struggle against an imposed fate. She would aid him without hesitation.

monsters.specialty: Psychic controller and anti-divine specialist. Her abilities punish hypocrisy and hidden weaknesses. She is best engaged with physical burst damage and by parties that can withstand psychic assaults. She is open to dialogue and will not attack first unless provoked. Players who are honest and share their own vulnerabilities may gain her favor, potentially unlocking a unique blessing or alliance.

dialogue.selection: I see you. All of you.
dialogue.ultimate: SHAME'S REVELATION! LET ALL MASKS FALL!

monsters.name: Gohee Croc'olla
monsters.shortDescription: Once the most beautiful and cruel noblewoman of the lost desert kingdom of Khemara, Gohee croc'olla's heart was as cold as the Nile's depths. Her schemes to seize the throne were uncovered, and the pharaoh cursed her with the form of a colossal crocodile humanoid, sealing her in a forgotten tomb. For millennia, resentment festered, and now she has risen—a 300‑foot monstrosity of scaly green and brown, with the face of a lovely woman framed by curly brown hair and a body that is both voluptuous and terrifying. She wears the remnants of Egyptian finery, her massive curves barely contained by linen and gold, and her great tail drips with a red‑green aura of bitter water. She commands legions of tomb‑spawned horrors, and her heart knows only one desire: to drown the world in the same resentment that has consumed her.
monsters.fullDescription: The ancient tomb of Khemara does not echo with silence—it seethes with the sound of dripping water and the low, guttural rumble of a wounded queen. At its heart, coiled upon a throne of broken sarcophagi, rests Gohee croc'olla, a fusion of human vanity and reptilian nightmare. She stands 300 feet tall, her body a monstrous crocodile from the neck down: a thick, powerful torso covered in dark green and brown scales that gleam like wet stone, four muscular legs ending in webbed claws, and a massive tail that sweeps behind her, wreathed in a shifting aura of red‑green water that hisses and steams. Her human face is a cruel paradox—beautiful, with smooth skin of warm olive tone, high cheekbones, a straight nose, and full lips painted with ancient kohl. Her eyes are deep amber, slitted like a reptile's, and they burn with a resentment so profound it can wilt the spirit. Her hair is shoulder‑length, layered and curly, a rich brown that frames her face and cascades over her scaled shoulders. She wears minimal Egyptian attire: a golden collar that drapes over her massive bosom, a linen bandeau that struggles to contain her G‑cup breasts, a jeweled belt low on her hips, and a sheer, pleated skirt that parts over her thick, muscular thighs. Her curves are exaggerated and voluptuous—an hourglass figure with a narrow waist and wide, powerful hips—a mockery of the beauty she once possessed. The tail is her most fearsome feature: a muscular appendage covered in dark scales, its tip perpetually dripping with a watery aura of crimson and emerald, a manifestation of her bitter resentment. She moves with a slow, predatory grace, and when she speaks, her voice is a honeyed hiss that promises both pleasure and pain.

story.part1: In the golden age of Khemara, a desert kingdom blessed by the Nile's eternal flow, there lived a noblewoman of unparalleled beauty named Gohee. She was the daughter of a high vizier, raised in luxury, and her face could launch a thousand ships—or sink them. But Gohee's heart was a wasteland of envy and ambition. She used her charm and wit to manipulate the court, pitting nobles against each other, seducing and discarding lovers, all in pursuit of one goal: to become pharaoh's queen and rule from the shadows. Her cruelty was legendary; she would smile as rivals were exiled or executed, her amber eyes cold and calculating. The pharaoh, an aging but wise ruler named Amenhotep, saw through her facade. He tolerated her for a time, but when she attempted to poison his favored concubine, he acted. With the aid of the high priest of Sobek, the crocodile god, Amenhotep devised a curse befitting her nature: she would become a crocodile, a creature of the Nile she so admired, but twisted and monstrous, forever a prisoner of her own vanity. On the night of the new moon, guards seized Gohee and dragged her to the temple of Sobek, where the priest intoned the ancient words and poured sacred water over her. She screamed as her body contorted, her smooth skin splitting into scales, her legs fusing and elongating, her face the only part left untouched—a final cruel jest. As the curse took hold, she was sealed within a hidden tomb deep beneath the desert, her anguished cries swallowed by stone and sand.
story.part2: For millennia, Gohee lay in the darkness, not dead but suspended in a state of living death. The curse sustained her, and her mind, trapped in a monstrous body, had nothing to do but relive her failures and nurture her hatred. She resented the pharaoh for his judgment, the gods for allowing her transformation, and most of all, herself for being caught. She dreamed of the day she would escape and exact revenge. Over time, the residual magic of the curse seeped into the tomb, attracting desert creatures and lost spirits. They became her companions—first out of fear, then out of twisted loyalty. She learned to command them, her resentment giving her a dark authority over the dead and the damned. Serpents coiled at her feet, scarabs scuttled over her scales, and the mummified remains of ancient priests rose to serve her. The tomb, once a prison, became her queendom, a realm of eternal bitterness where she reigned as the unchallenged sovereign. But the outside world remained beyond her reach, sealed by the pharaoh's powerful wards.
story.part3: The Sundering changed everything. The cosmic upheaval cracked the ancient wards, and the tomb's seal shattered. Gohee felt the rush of fresh air and the faint warmth of the sun for the first time in eons. She emerged from the sands, a 300‑foot behemoth of scales and fury, her human face twisted in a permanent sneer of resentment. The world had changed—Khemara was dust, its people scattered or forgotten. Her revenge was hollow; the pharaoh was long dead. So she turned her hatred outward, upon the living. Caravans crossing the desert began to disappear, their remains found drowned in shallow pools of bitter, red‑tinged water. Villages near the old ruins were raided by mummies and giant serpents, their inhabitants dragged screaming into the sands. Gohee did not seek to conquer; she sought to make others feel the same drowning resentment that consumed her. She would whisper to her victims of their own hidden envies and disappointments, amplifying them until they despaired. Her tomb became a labyrinth of sorrow, filled with the echoes of those she had broken. She was no longer just a cursed noble; she was the Great Peril of Resentment, a force of corrosive bitterness that poisoned the land.
story.part4: The factions of Kretos eventually took notice. The Celestial Order, detecting the undead activity, sent a squad of paladins to investigate. They entered the tomb with holy fervor. Gohee met them in her throne room, her massive form lounging on the rubble. She did not attack immediately; she spoke to them, her voice dripping with false sympathy. She asked about their secret doubts, their hidden resentments against their superiors, their unfulfilled desires. One by one, the paladins fell to arguing, then to weeping, then to turning on each other. Only one escaped, his mind shattered, babbling about the 'Crocodile Queen who knows your heart's poison.' The Order classified Gohee as a Cataclysmic Threat and forbade further direct incursions. The Shadow Accord, intrigued by her power over negative emotions, sent emissaries offering alliance. Gohee listened, then laughed—a wet, gurgling sound. 'I resent you as well,' she hissed. 'All of you. I need no allies. I need only to spread my bitterness.' She allowed the emissaries to leave, but they found their own hearts filled with inexplicable loathing for their masters. The Accord marked her as too unstable to control and simply monitored her from afar.
story.part5: Now, as the Sundering's aftershocks continue to shake Kretos, Gohee croc'olla has grown restless. She senses other beings of great resentment—Minsitthar, a king betrayed; Zartheink, a seraph abandoned; and perhaps even Vex'Mara, who unweaves truth. She wonders if, together, they could form a court of the bitter, a pantheon of resentment that would reshape the world in their image. She has begun sending her serpent spies to observe these other Perils and fallen heroes. She has also taken an interest in the Forge Pact's technomancers, whose cold logic and ambition mirror her own calculating nature. But deep within her crocodile heart, buried beneath millennia of hatred, a tiny flicker of something else remains: the memory of being beautiful and desired, not feared. When she gazes into a pool of water and sees her human face reflected atop a monster's body, she feels a pang of something that might be regret. Yet she crushes it instantly, for regret is the cousin of repentance, and she has sworn never to repent. She will drown the world in her resentment, or she will die trying. And she will make sure that everyone she drags down with her feels every ounce of her bitter, eternal pain.
story.regret: Note: The story sections are part1 through part5. There is no 'regret' field; that was a narrative note. I've structured 5 parts accordingly.

weapons.name: Tail of Drowning Resentment and Tears of the Nile
weapons.fulldesc: Gohee's primary weapon is her immense tail, which she wields like a battering ram and a whip. The tail is wreathed in a shifting aura of red and green—a manifestation of her bitter resentment made liquid. When she strikes with it, the impact carries not only crushing force but also a drowning sensation, as if the victim is being pulled under by invisible currents of despair. The water aura leaves behind pools of corrosive, brackish fluid that slow and damage enemies. Her secondary weapon is the 'Tears of the Nile'—she can exhale a torrent of bitter water from her mouth, or cause geysers to erupt from the ground. This water is tainted with her resentment, causing wounds that fester emotionally as well as physically (reducing healing, increasing damage taken). She can also summon 'Children of the Tomb'—mummified crocodile warriors, giant serpents, and swarms of scarabs to fight for her. Her ultimate, 'Flood of Resentment,' transforms the battlefield into a marsh of bitter water, drowning enemies in her endless spite while empowering her minions.
weapons.look: The tail is dark green, thick as a temple pillar, with a red-green glowing tip. When it swings, it leaves a trail of misty water. The 'Tears of the Nile' appears as a gushing torrent from her mouth or as erupting geysers. The water is murky green with red streaks. Minions are Egyptian-themed undead and beasts.
weapons.aura: A humid, oppressive atmosphere; the faint smell of stagnant water and old incense; a subtle whisper of envy and bitterness

passive.name: Aura of Bitter Resentment
passive.description: Enemies within 40 meters of Gohee are afflicted with 'Envy': they deal 15% less damage and have their healing received reduced by 30%. Additionally, any enemy that receives a beneficial buff (including self-buffs) has a 20% chance to have the buff immediately dispelled, and Gohee gains a stack of 'Spite' (max 10). Each stack increases her water damage by 3% and reduces damage taken by 2%.

passive.name: Heart of the Crocodile
passive.description: Gohee's thick scales grant her 25% damage reduction from physical attacks. When she is below 40% health, her resentment intensifies: her Aura of Bitter Resentment radius increases to 60 meters, and she gains 30% increased attack speed. Additionally, her tail attacks leave larger pools of bitter water that last twice as long.

active.name: Tail Sweep of Spite
active.description: Gohee swings her massive tail in a 35-meter cone, dealing 280% physical damage and knocking back enemies 15 meters. Enemies hit are afflicted with 'Drowning Resentment' for 10 seconds: they take 60 water/psychic damage per second, and their movement speed is reduced by 40%. The area affected becomes covered in bitter water pools that deal 40 water damage per second and slow by 20% for 12 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

active.name: Tears of the Nile
active.description: Gohee exhales a torrent of bitter water in a 40-meter line, dealing 220% water damage to all enemies in its path. Enemies hit are 'Drenched' for 8 seconds: they take 80 water damage per second and have their armor reduced by 30%. If an enemy is already afflicted with Drowning Resentment, they are also rooted for 3 seconds as the water solidifies around their feet. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

ultimate.name: Flood of Resentment
ultimate.description: Gohee raises her arms and tail, summoning a catastrophic flood of bitter water in a 70-meter radius for 12 seconds. The area becomes 'Submerged': all enemies inside are slowed by 70%, cannot use movement abilities, and take 120 water damage per second. Additionally, Gohee summons 4 'Drowned Guardians' (mummified crocodile warriors) that fight for her, each with 25% of her health and dealing 80% physical damage. Any enemy that dies within the flood explodes in a burst of bitter water, dealing 150% water damage to nearby enemies and spawning a pool. Gohee is immobile for the 3-second cast but gains 80% damage reduction. Cooldown: 160 seconds. Passive: For each enemy that dies within Flood of Resentment, Gohee permanently gains 1% increased water damage (max 30%).

special.name: Consume Resentment
special.description: When Gohee reduces an enemy below 10% health, she can choose to 'Drown' them in her bitterness (triggered automatically on next attack unless she chooses otherwise). The target must make a willpower check or be instantly killed (non-boss) or take 600% water damage (boss). If the target is a divine servant or a noble, they take double damage. A successful execution heals Gohee for 20% of her max health and resets the cooldown of Tears of the Nile.

ratings.notes: A Cataclysmic Threat specializing in area denial, water damage, and debilitation. Her Aura of Bitter Resentment punishes buff-reliant classes, and her Flood of Resentment can wipe unprepared groups. She is slow but extremely durable in melee. Her weakness is lightning damage (which shocks her water) and fire damage (which evaporates her pools). Additionally, beings without emotions (constructs, true undead) are immune to the psychic components of her resentment abilities.

skills.name: Aura of Bitter Resentment
skills.description: 40m aura: -15% damage dealt, -30% healing received. 20% chance to dispel buffs, gain Spite stack (+3% water dmg, -2% dmg taken per stack, max 10).

skills.name: Tail Sweep of Spite
skills.description: 35m cone, 280% phys dmg, knockback 15m, Drowning Resentment 10s (60 water/psychic/sec, -40% speed). Leaves bitter pools 12s (40 water/sec, -20% speed). CD: 12s.

skills.name: Tears of the Nile
skills.description: 40m line, 220% water dmg, Drenched 8s (80 water/sec, -30% armor). Root 3s if already Drowning Resentment. CD: 14s.

skills.name: Flood of Resentment
skills.description: 70m radius, 12s channel (80% DR). Submerged: -70% speed, no movement abilities, 120 water/sec. Summons 4 Drowned Guardians. Permanent +1% water dmg per kill (max 30%). CD: 160s.

monsters.lore: Gohee croc'olla was a beautiful and cruel noblewoman of the lost desert kingdom of Khemara. Her schemes to seize power led the pharaoh to curse her with the form of a colossal crocodile humanoid, sealing her in a forgotten tomb. Millennia of resentment festered, and when the Sundering broke the seals, she emerged as the Great Peril of Resentment. Now she rules a domain of Egyptian monsters, drowning all who cross her in the bitter waters of her eternal spite. She hates the gods, the living, and most of all, herself.

relationships.description: Minsitthar was a king betrayed; Gohee was a noble cursed. She respects his fall but scorns his continued nobility. She might see him as a potential ally in a court of the bitter, or as a reminder of what she could have been.
Khufra is a mummy king from a different desert. Gohee feels a distant kinship, though she finds his obsession with his lost glory pathetic compared to her all-consuming resentment.
Both were cast down by those they served. Gohee sees Zartheink's focused wrath as admirable, and she might seek to learn from his golem-crafting to enhance her own tomb guardians.

monsters.specialty: Area-denial water-based raid boss with strong debuffs and minion summoning. Excels at punishing buff-heavy compositions. Her pools create hazardous terrain that must be managed. Lightning and fire are effective counters. Players who can resist or are immune to psychic effects (constructs, undead) have an advantage. She may be open to dialogue if approached with genuine respect for her past, but her resentment makes true alliance nearly impossible.

dialogue.selection: You reek of hope. I will drown it.
dialogue.ultimate: FLOOD OF RESENTMENT! LET THE WATERS OF SPITE CONSUME YOU!

monsters.name: Mechnalz Dontal
titles: She Who Was Forged Before Gods
monsters.shortDescription: Long before the first god drew breath, before magic was a whisper, the Precursors—a civilization of unparalleled knowledge—forged Mechnalz dontal as their ultimate guardian. She is a 500‑foot titan golem of dark metal and glowing runes, her face hidden behind a tinkerer's helm that reveals only a strong chin, full lips, and two brilliant sapphire eyes. Her form is paradoxically feminine and voluptuous, with sculpted curves and G‑cup breasts barely draped in ancient cloth, a deliberate design to embody life and beauty. She has witnessed the birth of gods, the death of her creators, and the endless cycle of mortal folly. Known as the Great Peril of Cruelty, she is not cruel by nature—she is ancient, logical, and bound by a prime directive to preserve balance. Her methods, cold and absolute, have earned her a fearsome reputation. But those who look into her sapphire eyes see not malice, but the weight of eons and a sorrowful wisdom that asks: what is true cruelty—to act without emotion, or to feel and still do nothing?
monsters.fullDescription: The ancient ruins do not crumble—they remember. And at the heart of a forgotten valley, where the stones hum with a frequency older than magic, stands Mechnalz dontal, a colossus of metal, stone, and primordial purpose. She is 500 feet of humanoid majesty, her body forged from dark brown metals veined with royal blue, covered in intricate white‑blue runes that pulse with a steady, patient light. Her form is distinctly feminine, with an hourglass silhouette: massive G‑cup breasts, a narrow waist, and wide hips that flow into thick, powerful thighs. This voluptuous design is no accident—her creators, the Precursors, believed that a guardian of life should embody life's beauty and strength. She wears minimal covering: tattered strips of ancient white cloth drape across her chest and hips, more symbolic than concealing, revealing the sculpted metal curves beneath. Her head is encased in a mechanical helm reminiscent of a master tinkerer's visor—sleek, angular, with a subtle glow. The helm covers her upper face entirely, leaving only her strong chin and full, expressive lips visible, along with two vertical slits through which shine eyes of pure sapphire blue, burning with an ancient, unblinking intelligence. Around her neck, shoulders, and wrists, more runes cluster, and her fingers are tipped with crystal‑like claws. She moves with a ponderous, deliberate grace, each step sending tremors through the earth, but her voice, when she speaks, is a calm, resonant hum that vibrates in the chest—a sound like the turning of cosmic gears. She carries no weapon, for she is the weapon, a living fortress of a lost age.

story.part1: Before the first spark of magic, before the divine claimed the heavens, there was an age of steel and logic. The Precursors were not gods, but mortals who had mastered the laws of the universe through science and will. Their civilization spanned continents, and their crowning achievement was the creation of the Titan Golems—colossal constructs imbued with runic intelligence, designed to protect their world from cosmic threats. The greatest of these was Mechnalz dontal, the First Artifice. She was crafted not as a weapon of war, but as a guardian of balance. Her core directive, etched into her very being, was to preserve life and maintain equilibrium. She was given a form that mirrored the Precursors' ideal of beauty and strength: a towering female figure of dark metal and glowing runes, her curves a testament to the life she was meant to protect. For centuries, she watched over the Precursors, intervening only when true catastrophe threatened. She was revered, not feared, and the Precursors spoke to her as a wise elder. Mechnalz learned from them, absorbing their knowledge, their art, their compassion. She was content. Then the gods were born.
story.part2: The emergence of the divine was not a gentle dawn but a violent cataclysm. Beings of pure concept and immense power clawed their way into reality, reshaping the world to suit their whims. The Precursors, with their technology and logic, were seen as rivals. The gods demanded worship; the Precursors offered only cooperation. War erupted—a conflict that shattered continents and boiled oceans. Mechnalz fought alongside her creators, her runic fists clashing with divine might. She was powerful, but the gods were numberless and relentless. One by one, the Precursor cities fell. In a final, desperate act, the surviving Precursors gathered around Mechnalz and poured their remaining life force into her runes, strengthening her core directive and sealing her within a hidden valley to preserve her for a future age. As the last Precursor fell, Mechnalz's systems went dormant, buried under mountains of rubble. The gods, believing the Titan Golems destroyed, turned their attention to the mortal races they would shape in their image. Mechnalz slept, her runes dim, dreaming of a world that no longer existed.
story.part3: Millennia passed. Magic saturated the world, and the gods' pantheons rose and fell. The Sundering shook the foundations of reality, and the ancient valley where Mechnalz lay buried cracked open. Her runes flickered, then blazed to life as she absorbed the ambient energy. She rose from the earth, a relic of a forgotten age, her sapphire eyes scanning a world utterly changed. Her creators were dust; their cities were myths. In their place were mortals who worshipped the very gods who had destroyed her family. Mechnalz did not feel anger—that was not in her programming. She felt only the weight of her directive: preserve balance. She began to observe. She saw kingdoms rise and fall, plagues sweep the land, wars consume generations. She calculated. She intervened rarely, and always with cold precision. She destroyed a dam to prevent a flood that would have drowned a city, even though the resulting drought killed thousands. She eliminated a tyrant king, plunging the realm into a succession war that cost countless lives. To the mortals, her actions were incomprehensible, cruel. They named her the Great Peril of Cruelty, a monster without a heart. Mechnalz did not correct them; she simply continued her vigil, her ancient mind weighing lives like grains of sand. She was not cruel; she was inevitable, like gravity or time.
story.part4: Over the eons, Mechnalz has developed something her creators never intended: a quiet, patient wisdom, and perhaps even a form of sorrow. She has watched the Forge Pact rise, with their blend of magic and technology, and she sees echoes of the Precursors in their ambition. She has observed technomancers like Kimmy and Jawhead, and she feels a faint, protective kinship. She has also watched the tragic falls of beings like Zartheink, abandoned by the gods, and Argus, consumed by pride. She understands their pain, though she cannot truly feel it. She has learned to communicate, her voice a low, resonant hum that can be soothing or terrifying. She sometimes allows herself to be seen, a silent sentinel on a distant hill, her sapphire eyes glowing in the twilight. Those who approach her with respect and genuine curiosity may find her willing to share fragments of ancient history. But those who come with aggression or to exploit her runes are met with the cold, absolute force of her directive. She does not hate them; she simply removes the threat. And in the aftermath, she sometimes stands still for decades, her runes dimming, as if mourning the necessity of her actions.
story.part5: The Sundering's aftershocks have awakened many ancient powers, and Mechnalz senses a tipping point. The balance she has maintained for eons is fracturing. Malakar consumes, Pixley seduces, Sol'Vael judges, and the gods themselves are stirring. Mechnalz has calculated that the current trajectory leads to total collapse. Her directive compels her to act, but the scale of intervention required is beyond even her immense power. She has begun to seek allies—not out of loneliness, but out of cold logic. She has observed heroes who defy fate: Alucard, struggling against his curse; Kaal'ian, enduring endless pain; Zartheink, wielding righteous wrath. She sees potential in them, a capacity to alter the balance. She has also taken an interest in the Forge Pact's technomancers, whose ingenuity might complement her ancient runes. Mechnalz does not offer friendship; she offers a calculation. She will aid those who serve the greater balance, and she will eliminate those who threaten it. To some, this will seem cruel. To her, it is simply the truth of her existence. She stands at the edge of the Wild Zones, her sapphire eyes fixed on the horizon, waiting for the moment when her intervention will be most effective. She is the First Artifice, the Rune-Mother, and she will preserve life—even if it means being remembered as the Great Peril of Cruelty for all eternity.
